commit.message:
perf(maglev): extend range analysis to handle Generic* arithmetic ops

Range analysis induction variable detection and forward propagation
now recognize GenericIncrement, GenericDecrement, GenericAdd,
GenericSubtract, and GenericMultiply — not just CheckedSmi* variants.

After global promotion replaces LoadGlobal/StoreGlobal with Phi nodes,
loop counter increments become GenericIncrement(Phi). Previously these
were invisible to range analysis, so they kept overflow checks (JO deopt).
Now they are converted to Int32Increment when the computed range fits i32.

Similarly, accumulator patterns like sum += value are detected through
GenericAdd, enabling Int32Add lowering when total accumulation is bounded.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Replace cubemap array shadows with 2D shadow atlas

Replace GL_TEXTURE_CUBE_MAP_ARRAY shadow maps with a single GL_TEXTURE_2D
shadow atlas. Each light gets a 3x2 tile block (6 cubemap faces) at an
index-based grid position, with per-face glViewport and per-light
scissored glClear.

This eliminates all glFramebufferTextureLayer calls during the shadow
pass. On macOS, each FBO attachment change triggered a Metal render pass
transition (endRenderPass -> submitCommandBuffer -> kernel roundtrip ->
beginRenderPass), which accounted for ~32% of total CPU time. The atlas
approach keeps the driver in a single render pass for the entire shadow
phase.

Key changes:
- r_shadow.c: Single FBO with 2D depth atlas, viewport/scissor per tile
- r_light.c: Populate per-light shadow atlas UV metadata in UBO
- light.glsl: Manual cubemap face selection + 2D PCF shadow sampling
- uniforms.glsl: sampler2DShadow replaces samplerCubeArrayShadow
- r_shadow_tile_size cvar (default 256) replaces r_shadow_cubemap_array_size
- Shadow caching preserved via stable index-based tile placement

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
refactor: normalise spec constructors to consistent 2-ctor pattern (#93)

## Phase 6: Consistent specification constructor patterns

### Goal
Normalise all spec constructors to a consistent 2-constructor pattern:
one full-DI constructor (for tests) and one parameterless constructor
(for JSON deserialisation) that chains to it with defaults.

### Changes

#### Constructor cleanup
- **`LinkTaskSpecification`** — removed intermediate `(IFileManagement)`
constructor
- **`CopyTaskSpecification`** — removed intermediate `(IFileManagement)`
constructor
- **`CloneGitRepositoryTaskSpecification`** — collapsed 4 constructors
to 2; parameterless now chains to full-DI
- **`LinkExpandTaskSpecification`** — removed intermediate
`(IFileSystem)` constructor

#### New interfaces
- **`IGitRunnerFactory`** / **`GitRunnerFactory`** — replaces the
`this.gitRunner ?? new ProcessGitRunner(logger)` null-coalescing
anti-pattern. The factory creates `ProcessGitRunner` with the real
logger at execution time.
- **`IEnvironmentVariableWriter`** / **`EnvironmentVariableWriter`** —
wraps `Environment.SetEnvironmentVariable`, making
`EnvironmentVariableTaskSpecification` testable without real env-var
side effects

#### Factory wiring
- **`ShellTaskSpecification.ExecuteAsync`** — now uses the injected
`IShellInvokerFactory` instead of an inline `switch` expression
(completes Phase 3 wiring that wasn't applied during merge)

### Verification

commit.message:
fix(maglev): zero register file in emit_direct_call_0 to prevent SIGSEGV

Add rep stosq register file zeroing to both the mono-cache HIT and
cache MISS paths in emit_direct_call_0.  The other direct-call emitters
(emit_direct_call_1, emit_direct_call_2) already zeroed theirs, but
emit_direct_call_0 skipped it under the assumption that JIT callees
always initialise every slot before reading.  In the closure_counter_1k
benchmark, stale stack memory in the uninitialized register file caused
the callee to dereference a garbage context pointer before the prologue
could store RSI, triggering a SIGSEGV on Linux.

HIT path: RAX/RCX/RDI are dead after the BA save/push sequence, so the
zeroing needs no register save/restore.

MISS path: R10 (ctx_ptr) and R11 (entry point) are live, so they are
push/popped around the rep stosq, matching the pattern in
emit_direct_call_1 and emit_direct_call_2.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Migrate CloudChat from MediaPipe to HuggingFace Transformers.js

Implements all 9 phases of the MIGRATION.md guide:

Phase 1: Remove MediaPipe LlmInference/FilesetResolver imports
Phase 2: Replace MODELS with 3 HuggingFace ONNX models (gemma3-1b, gemma4-e2b, gemma4-e4b)
Phase 3: Add inference Web Worker system (createInferenceWorker, attachWorkerHandlers, loadModel, generateOnce, stopGeneration)
Phase 4: Rewrite handleSend with generateOnce(), add buildContextMessages/formatToolResponse, change history format from {role,text} to {role,content}
Phase 5: Add multimodal input UI (camera/mic buttons, camera overlay modal)
Phase 6: Add prepareAttachmentsForWorker, camera capture, mic recording handlers
Phase 7: Add buildAgentSystemPrompt with JSON schema tool injection
Phase 8: Add extractThinking for thinking block extraction
Phase 9: Add model selector in settings, replace initGemma with loadModel startup

* feat: §29.8.5 cache keying by project (#265)

Add project-scoped state caching for modules to prevent cross-project
data leakage. Module state (list data, filters, scroll positions, etc.)
is keyed by moduleId+projectId and automatically switches when the
active project changes.

New SDK hook: useProjectScopedState(key, initialValue) — works like
useState but the value is keyed by moduleId+projectId+key. Previous
project's cached values are preserved for instant restore on switch-back.

Implementation uses nested Map<moduleId, Map<projectId, Map<key, value>>>
to avoid composite-string key pitfalls. Cache entries are cleaned up on
module dispose (both inline and via registry._disposeSuspended()).

* fix: persist DataProtection key ring across Docker rebuilds (#269)

AddDataProtection() was called without PersistKeysToFileSystem(),
so the key ring regenerated on every container rebuild. This caused:
- Session cookies to become undecryptable (logout)
- Encrypted OAuth secrets in system_config to fail Unprotect()
- Users prompted to re-run the GitHub OAuth setup wizard

Now explicitly persists keys to ~/.aspnet/DataProtection-Keys,
which is volume-mounted as matrix-keys in docker-compose.yml.
Includes a fallback guard for edge cases where UserProfile is empty.

Also recovered the old key (99f8aade) from the stale
the-matrix_matrix-keys volume into the active docker_matrix-keys
volume, so existing encrypted secrets will decrypt again after
container restart.

detects the version change and migrates settings:

- Remove stored values incompatible with new schema constraints
  (type changes, out-of-range numbers, removed select options)

- Preserve unmapped fields as-is (keys not in new schema)
- Do NOT eagerly persist defaults — preserves §29.5.9 provenance
- On failure: log warning, skip version update, retry next startup
- Secret values skipped (masked, can't validate)

DeleteKeyAsync. Infrastructure: SqliteModuleSettingsStore implements them.
ModuleDiscoveryService runs migration after scan.

* fix: pin Docker Compose project name to prevent volume splitting

Running `docker compose` from different directories (e.g. `docker/` vs a
deployment dir) created separate named volumes, causing the OAuth config
stored in SQLite to be stranded in one volume while the app ran against a
different empty volume — prompting the setup wizard on every restart.

Changes:

  is stable regardless of which directory `docker compose` runs from
- Add automatic volume migration to update.sh that detects orphaned
  volumes from previous project names and copies data + encryption keys

* fix: unwrap JSON-quoted strings in plugin GetAllAsync

Plugin settings stored non-secret string values as JSON (e.g. hello → "hello").
GetAllAsync returned the raw JSON, so the frontend displayed values with quotes.
On the next save, the controller JSON-serialized the already-quoted value, adding
another layer of quotes on every save cycle.

Added UnwrapJsonString() to GetAllAsync that strips JSON string quoting before
returning. Numbers and booleans pass through unchanged. This matches the pattern
already used in McpConfigMerger.ResolveVariableReferences (line 433).

Added round-trip test proving save→read→save no longer double-quotes.
